data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260515T083655-a4795d11/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260515T083655-a4795d11/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260515T083655-a4795d11/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260515T083655-a4795d11/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260515T083655-a4795d11/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-08b452ee">
    <title>work: fail fast on stale DDx source checkout binary</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Operators can run `ddx work` from the DDx source checkout with an installed binary built from an older commit. That produced live workers still using old readiness/routing behavior even after the local source tree had been fixed. `ddx version` can warn, but `ddx work` and `ddx try` still proceed to claim beads and perform actual builds with stale code.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/cmd/command_factory.go:603` implements `warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource`, but it is only called from the `version` command path at `cli/cmd/command_factory.go:511`.
- `cli/cmd/command_factory.go:169` runs the global pre-run version gate, but it checks `.ddx/versions.yaml` semver only and does not block a stale installed binary inside the DDx source repository.
- `cli/cmd/work.go:130` and `cli/cmd/try.go:116` resolve the project root for execution, but neither refuses to build from an older DDx binary when the project root is the DDx source repo.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an execution preflight for `ddx work` and `ddx try`: when running inside the DDx source repository, and the binary reports a concrete build commit that is an ancestor of the source checkout `HEAD` with one or more commits ahead, fail before queue scan, claim, readiness, or dispatch. The error must name the binary commit, source `HEAD`, project root, and the exact recovery command (`make install` from the source checkout). Keep `ddx version`, `ddx doctor`, `ddx init`, `ddx upgrade`, `help`, and `completion` usable for recovery. Do not make this a warning for worker commands.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/cmd/command_factory.go`
- `cli/cmd/work.go`
- `cli/cmd/try.go`
- `cli/cmd/version_gate_test.go`
- `cli/cmd/work_test.go`
- `cli/cmd/try_test.go`

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change release semver gating against `.ddx/versions.yaml`.
- Do not auto-run `make install` or mutate the operator's PATH.
- Do not block dev binaries with `Version=dev` or binaries without a known commit.
- Do not inspect workers from other projects; this preflight is scoped to the resolved project root.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestWork_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeQueueScan|TestTry_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeClaim|TestVersionWarnsStaleSourceBinary|TestCheckVersionGate' -count=1` passes.
2. `TestWork_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeQueueScan` proves no bead is claimed and no readiness/run service call occurs when the DDx source tree is ahead of the binary commit.
3. `TestTry_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeClaim` proves `ddx try <id>` fails before claim/attempt evidence when the same stale-source condition is present.
4. `ddx work` and `ddx try` error text includes the project root, binary commit, source HEAD, and `make install` recovery instruction.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:cli, area:execution, kind:bug, operator-ux, stale-binary</labels>
    <metadata spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="6e19bce477fe8f980c98824a13bfdbd040d157b2" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260515T083655-a4795d11"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented the stale-source guard for `ddx work` and `ddx try`. The shared detection lives in `cli/cmd/command_factory.go`, `runWork` and `runTry` now fail before queue scan or claim when the installed binary commit is behind the DDx source checkout `HEAD`, and the error text includes the project root, binary commit, source `HEAD`, and `cd <root> && make install`. `ddx version` still warns instead of blocking, but now carries the same structured detail.

Added regressions in `cli/cmd/version_gate_test.go`, `cli/cmd/work_test.go`, and `cli/cmd/try_test.go` covering the warning path and the early-failure/no-claim/no-dispatch behavior. Verified with `cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestWork_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeQueueScan|TestTry_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeClaim|TestVersionWarnsStaleSourceBinary|TestVersion_StaleWarning_PrintsToStderr|TestCheckVersionGate' -count=1` and `lefthook run pre-commit`. Commit: `9011785f9` (`fix(cli): fail fast on stale source checkout binary [ddx-08b452ee]`).
